column1: # Pinned prebuilt JSCOnly artifacts for the source backend.
# build.rs (rong_jscore_sys) reads this file. When the source backend has no
# artifact resolved (no RONG_JSC_* env, nothing in the cache), it downloads the
# row matching the current target triple, verifies its sha256, and extracts it
# into the per-target cache (<cache>/<target>/{include,lib}). Download is a quick
# curl + tar; disable it with RONG_JSC_DOWNLOAD=0.
# Once rows are populated, this is what makes the source backend "just work" on
# Linux and Windows with no local WebKit build. Rows should point at vanilla
# JSCOnly artifacts: static archives off-Apple, a JavaScriptCore.framework on
# Apple.
# Format (whitespace-separated), one row per target:
#   <target-triple>            <release-tag>        <filename>                                   <sha256-hex>
# Download URL = ${RONG_JSC_ARTIFACT_BASE_URL:-https://github.com/LingXia-Dev/Rong/releases/download}/<tag>/<filename>
# To produce rows: run the "Build JSC artifacts" workflow
# (.github/workflows/build-jsc-artifacts.yml), or locally run
# `scripts/build_jsc_artifact.sh --package <file>.tar.gz`. Both print the row
# (target, release-tag, filename, sha256) to paste below.
# No rows yet → source builds require a manually-provided artifact. Example of a
# populated row (commented out):
